commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=49b6ff2e11b626b832b8c3cbdf1e5957c96eda5e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch restacks the structured doctor repair contract work and converts legacy plugin manifests, configured plugin installs, and plugin registry maintenance to structured detect/repair/dry-run/effects/detect-after flows with focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. The disabled registry accounting issue is source-reproducible on the PR head: detect emits a disabled finding, repair returns no status, and the runner treats missing status as repaired before validation.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed to refresh current-head real behavior proof, and the protected draft/stack order also requires maintainer handling before any repair or merge automation.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Goal

Convert the plugin/config repair bucket to the structured doctor contract so package/config/state repairs are detectable, previewable, and validated through the same path.

This PR exists to make plugin-registry and configured-plugin install maintenance auditable from first principles:

- detect is read-only and silent
- repair reports concrete config/package/state effects
- dry-run describes real work without fabricating diffs
- non-dry-run repair is followed by scoped detect-after validation

## Scope

| Check | Goal | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests` | Move legacy manifest capability metadata into the current contract shape. | Detect finds legacy top-level capability keys, including manifests reached through configured `plugins.load.paths`; repair rewrites only detected manifests. |
| `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs` | Ensure configured plugin/channel installs are accounted for after release updates. | Detect reports stale release repair metadata; repair installs configured plugin/channel packages and stamps release metadata. |
| `core/doctor/plugin-registry` | Make registry/package repair a structured state repair instead of a print-oriented helper. | Detect reports stale registry/package/peer-link issues; repair refreshes registry/package state without terminal printing. |

## Structured Repair Matrix

| Check | Detect / lint behavior | Repair behavior | Dry-run / diff / effects | Detect-after proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests` | Reads manifests and reports legacy top-level capability keys as findings. | Rewrites only detected manifest migrations under `contracts`. | Concrete file diff plus file effect: `would-rewrite-legacy-plugin-manifest-contracts` / `rewrite-legacy-plugin-manifest-contracts`. | Scoped validation confirms legacy top-level capability keys are gone after rewrite, including configured plugin load paths. |
| `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs` | Reports stale release repair metadata when configured plugin installs need the release repair marker. | Installs configured plugin/channel packages as before and stamps release metadata. | Config diff for `meta`; package effects for plugin/channel installs; config effect for `meta.lastTouchedVersion`. | Scoped validation confirms current release metadata no longer reports the configured install finding. |
| `core/doctor/plugin-registry` | `detectPluginRegistryStateIssues(...)` is read-only and silent. | Converted repair calls `repairPluginRegistryState(...)` directly; legacy `maybeRepairPluginRegistryState(...)` remains the print wrapper for old non-fix paths. | State/package effects for registry refresh, stale managed package removal, and peer-link repair; no synthetic diff for package-manager work. | Registry/package tests assert package directory, lockfile, registry record, peer-link state, and clean detect-after. |
| Note-phase contract | No terminal output from structured detect. | Structured repair returns `{ config, changes, warnings }` and does not call `note(...)`; no bespoke note sink is used. | Presentation stays owned by the base runner/contribution layer. | Autoreview on the restacked head confirms the bucket follows the finalized note contract. |

## Proof

| Proof | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Head | `da75d3d1cb7a` on `doctor-next-plugin-registry`, stacked on #83753. |
| Focused plugin manifest/registry/core/repair tests | `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed, 5 files / 70 tests. |
| Core typecheck | `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` passed. |
| Formatting / whitespace | `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts` and `git diff --check doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance..HEAD` passed. |
| Autoreview | `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot"` passed with no accepted/actionable findings. |
| Earlier Crabbox proof | Build, focused tests, release configured-plugin tests, tsgo, and real doctor CLI lint proof passed in run `run_40f3018e5b28`. |

## Out of Scope

- Adding new public CLI flags.
- Changing plugin install policy.
- Treating package-manager work as if it had a file diff when no stable before/after file exists.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Structured doctor repair conversion for configured plugin install and plugin registry flows; lint no longer reports configured installs once the release repair marker is current; real CLI lint reports the intended structured checks.

Real environment tested: WSL source worktree `/tmp/openclaw-doctor-next` for restack/focused proof, plus earlier direct AWS Crabbox lease `cbx_95f6e9c97891` (`silver-crab`) for real CLI lint proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused doctor tests, core tsgo, oxfmt check, diff check, autoreview with parallel focused tests, and earlier Crabbox build/focused tests/release configured-plugin tests/real `openclaw doctor --lint --only ... --json` checks against synthetic old/current config metadata and empty registry state.

Evidence after fix: Restacked head `da75d3d1cb7a` passed local focused proof and autoreview. Crabbox run `run_40f3018e5b28` exited 0. Proof images are posted in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84290#issuecomment-4491556493 and stored in https://gist.github.com/giodl73-repo/e41418e607535f7d29451ff7329e8e00.

Observed result after fix: configured `plugins.load.paths` manifests are included in detect/repair validation; old configured install config reports `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs`; current release marker returns clean; empty registry reports `core/doctor/plugin-registry`; focused tests, typecheck, formatting, whitespace, and autoreview passed.

What was not tested: package, Docker, and update-survivor lanes were not rerun for this narrow structured repair slice.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
